search_candidates.title: AADI profit falls 37% to USD 760 million in 2025 | IDNFinancials
search_candidates.content: ## AADI profit falls 37% to USD 760 million in 2025. JAKARTA — PT Adaro Andalan Indonesia Tbk (AADI), a coal company under the Adaro Group that operates seven mines in Kalimantan, posted net profit of USD 760 million, or around IDR 12.8 trillion, in the 2025 financial year. The figure represents a 37% year-on-year decline, compared with profit of USD 1.21 billion recorded by AADI in 2024. According to its financial statement, the decline in AADI’s net profit was in line with operating revenue falling 7.69% to USD 4.91 billion in 2025. The company also recorded a significant drop in net other income, which fell 91.5% to USD 28 million in 2025. Meanwhile, the company’s coal production increased 4.42% year on year to 68.73 million tonnes, while the stripping ratio — the ratio of overburden removal in mining areas — stood at 4.24 times. With the 2025 earnings performance, AADI’s earnings per share stood at USD 0.09762, equivalent to IDR 1,648, assuming the JISDOR exchange rate of IDR 16,879 per USD today.
